keyPoints: Center constraint-based AI workflows (generation quotas, independent recall, and mandatory justification) as core mechanisms for testing creative quality and risk-taking.
Articulate boundary conditions by domain and user experience to explain when constraints boost versus hinder creativity.
Make HITL governance and explicit verification essential drivers of quality, accountability, and trust in constraint designs.

Differentiate expected effects by domain risk profile: high-stakes domains demand explainability and rigorous verification; creative domains accept more exploratory constraint patterns with governance.
Highlight differential user-experience effects: novices are more vulnerable to overreliance; experts may need lighter or more tailored constraint regimes.
Provide actionable design knobs and measurement plans: domain-specific quotas, transparent disclosures, diverse output sampling, and creativity plus risk metrics to guide deployment.
